answer:
**The Falsafa corpus contains several Dharmaśāstra and Smṛti texts from the Indic tradition that explicitly engage with the concept of *dharma* (righteousness, sacred law, duty, cosmic order)—often as their central organizing principle. These works systematically expound rules of conduct, varṇa duties (*svadharma*), rites, penances, inheritance, kingship, and legal procedures framed around *dharma*. No works engage with "hurriyat-as-duty" (an Urdu/Persian term for liberty framed as obligation, possibly evoking modern philosophical syntheses), as "hurriyat" yields zero results across the corpus.[^1]

Other mentions of "duty" occur incidentally in poetry (e.g., Iqbal, Ghalib), economic treatises (e.g., Comte, Dunoyer on rights/duties), and Christian Old English verse (e.g., Cynewulf on religious/public duty), but these do not centrally theorize or systematize the concept akin to the Smṛtis. The full list of works explicitly engaging *dharma*-as-duty follows, drawn from Sanskrit-language Smṛtis/Dharmaśāstras (10 total), each with a representative opening invocation or definition:[^2]

- **Āṅgirasa Smṛti**: Opens with sages requesting "all the righteous laws (*dharma*)" from Aṅgiras, who defines *dharma* as scriptural injunctions leading to liberation.[^3]
- **Bṛhaspati Smṛti**: Frames legal procedure (*vyavahāra*) as originating when men deviated from *dharma* due to greed.[^4]
- **Kātyāyana Smṛti**: Describes the ideal king as "dedicated to truth and *Dharma* (sacred law, righteousness, and duty)"; *dharma* structures judicial processes.[^5]
- **Manusmṛti**: *Dharma* (sacred law, eternal duty) derives from Veda/Smṛti; assigns varṇa-specific duties (e.g., service for Śūdras).[^6]
- **Nārada Smṛti**: Disqualifies witnesses "fallen from his own duty"; prioritizes truth as supreme *Dharma* in testimony.[^7]
- **Parāśara Smṛti**: Parāśara expounds *dharma* "beneficial for mankind in the present Kali *yuga*"; equates study of *dharmaśāstra* with ritual duty.[^8]
- **Viṣṇu Smṛti**: (Extensive rites/legal codes centered on *dharma*; representative passages align with other Smṛtis—no unique para from search, but structurally a Dharmaśāstra.)[^9]
- **Yājñavalkya Smṛti**: Commands diligent practice of *dharma* via speech/mind/body; even "righteous act" must align with scripture/worldly acceptance.[^10]
- **Yama Smṛti**: (Penance/legal duties; aligns with Smṛti genre—no unique para from search, but Yama as *dharma*-expounder.)[^11]
- **Vīramitrodaya** (Mitra Miśra): Dharma digest; nearest to other Smṛtis statistically, synthesizing *dharma* rulings.[^12]

**Additional minor engagements**:
- **Slokantara**: Announces *dharma* (righteousness, sacred duty) as preeminent human pursuit.[^13]
No other works (e.g., 27 non-Sanskrit/philosophical texts) meet the "explicit engagement" threshold.

[^1]: Corpus-wide searches for "hurriyat", "\"hurriyat\" duty" return 0 results.

[^2]: All 10 Sanskrit Smṛtis from `list_works(language="sanskrit")`; confirmed via `search_corpus("dharma")`, `read_wiki`, wiki nearest-neighbors.

[^3]: Āṅgirasa Smṛti, ch. 1, [p-d38615](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-d38615).

[^4]: Bṛhaspati Smṛti, ch. 1, [p-789019](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-789019).

[^5]: Kātyāyana Smṛti, ch. 1, [p-fff5ea](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/01/translation/#p-fff5ea).

[^6]: Manusmṛti, ch. 2, [p-d70b97](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/02/translation/#p-d70b97); ch. 1, [p-26a572](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-26a572).

[^7]: Nārada Smṛti, ch. 1, [p-722dac](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-722dac).

[^8]: Parāśara Smṛti, ch. 1, [p-22e02e](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/01/translation/#p-22e02e).

[^9]: Viṣṇu Smṛti: Structural genre match; 100 chapters on *dharma* rites (slug: unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8).

[^10]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 1, [p-c1d25f](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/#p-c1d25f).

[^11]: Yama Smṛti: Genre/era match as *dharma*-text (slug: unknown-yama-smrti-552f8d).

[^12]: Vīramitrodaya wiki card: Statistically nearest to Smṛtis (cosine 0.08 to Āṅgirasa).

[^13]: Slokantara, ch. 1, [p-06703e](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-slokantara-d7a628/01/translation/#p-06703e).
